decision: Use --thinking-mode CLI flag in ta-agent-ollama args instead of extra TOML field
rationale: AgentFrameworkManifest does not have an 'extra' or 'metadata' map field. Adding one would require a schema change to a shared crate. Passing --thinking-mode true/false directly in the profile's args list keeps all configuration in args, is parsed by clap's Option<bool>, and requires no struct changes.
alternatives: Add HashMap<String, toml::Value> extra field to AgentFrameworkManifest
Store as separate profile-level fields requiring new serde deserialization logic

decision: Inline bundled Qwen3.5 profile TOML as string constants in agent.rs instead of include_str!
rationale: include_str! with a relative path from apps/ta-cli/src/commands/agent.rs to the agents/ directory at the workspace root would be fragile (deep relative path, breaks if file layout changes). Inline string constants are compile-time checked and self-contained. The agents/ TOML files are still created for file-level discovery, but the install command uses the constants.
alternatives: Read from disk at runtime and bundle in the binary via build.rs

decision: Place resolve_model_auto as a standalone async fn rather than on OllamaClient
rationale: resolve_model_auto needs to be called before the final OllamaClient is constructed (since the model name is part of client init). Making it a standalone fn that takes base_url as &str keeps the responsibility boundary clean: client.rs is for HTTP mechanics, main.rs handles argument resolution logic.

decision: Read plan phase status from staging workspace BEFORE auto_clean in draft.rs
rationale: The original code read from target_dir after auto_clean could delete the staging dir, causing a false-positive 'still pending' warning even when the agent correctly marked the phase done. The fix reads from goal.workspace_path (staging) before auto_clean runs, then falls back to target_dir only for the validation step if staging status is non-Done.
alternatives: Only read from target_dir after apply has copied files
